rddid 0.3.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Changelog

CCT (MSE-optimal) bandwidth for a single local-linear RD

Source: R/bandwidth.R
rd_bw_cct.Rd

Returns the Calonico–Cattaneo–Titiunik MSE-optimal bandwidths h and b for a single local-linear RD using rdrobust. Falls back gracefully when rdrobust is unavailable, the call fails, or the returned bandwidth is non-positive/non-finite.

rd_bw_cct(y, x, c = 0, p = 1L, kernel = "triangular")

Arguments

y

Outcome vector.

x

Running variable vector.

c

Cutoff (default 0).

p

Polynomial order (default 1L, local linear).

kernel

Kernel type: "triangular" (default), "epanechnikov", or "uniform".

Value

A named numeric vector c(h = ..., b = ...) with the main and pilot bandwidths. When the CCT computation is unavailable, both equal 0.5 * IQR(x) (or sd(x) if IQR is zero), and a message is emitted naming the reason.

Contents

Developed by Dor Leventer, Daniel Nevo.
